title: San Francisco Sourdough Boule

seo.title: How to Make San Francisco Sourdough Boule — Easy American Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic San Francisco Sourdough Boule. This easy American recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
longTailKeywords: how to make San Francisco Sourdough Boule
easy San Francisco Sourdough Boule recipe
authentic American San Francisco Sourdough Boule
lowCompetitionKeywords: American San Francisco Sourdough Boule recipe
seo.h1: Authentic San Francisco Sourdough Boule Recipe
h2Headings: What is San Francisco Sourdough Boule?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Authentic American San Francisco Sourdough Boule recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade San Francisco Sourdough Boule — a beloved American classic

description: Experience the iconic taste of San Francisco with this homemade Sourdough Boule. This rustic, crusty bread boasts a distinct tangy flavor and a beautifully chewy interior, perfect for any meal. Its signature crackly crust and airy crumb make it a beloved American baking classic.

instructions.title: Mix the Dough
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the active sourdough starter and lukewarm water, stirring until the starter is mostly dissolved. Add the bread flour, whole wheat flour, and sea salt. Mix with your hands or a Danish dough whisk until no dry flour remains and a shaggy dough forms. Cover the bowl and let rest for 30 minutes for autolyse.

instructions.title: Bulk Fermentation and Folds
instructions.text: After the autolyse, perform a series of stretch and folds. Every 30 minutes for the next 2 hours, gently stretch a portion of the dough up and fold it over itself, rotating the bowl until all sides have been folded. Cover and let the dough bulk ferment at room temperature for a total of 4-6 hours, or until it has increased in volume by about 30-50% and feels airy.

instructions.title: Pre-Shape and Bench Rest
instructions.text: Lightly flour your work surface. Gently turn the dough out onto the floured surface. Carefully shape the dough into a loose round, being careful not to degas it too much. Cover with a kitchen towel and let it rest for 20-30 minutes. This helps the gluten relax for the final shaping.

instructions.title: Final Shaping and Cold Proof
instructions.text: Dust a banneton or a bowl lined with a floured kitchen towel. Gently shape the dough into a tight boule, creating tension on the surface. Place the shaped dough seam-side up into the prepared banneton. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for 12-18 hours for the cold proof. This develops flavor and makes the dough easier to handle.

instructions.title: Preheat Oven and Dutch Oven
instructions.text: About an hour before baking, place a Dutch oven with its lid into your oven and preheat to 475°F (245°C). Ensure the oven and Dutch oven are fully preheated before proceeding. This high heat creates a perfect steaming environment for a crusty boule.

instructions.title: Score and Bake
instructions.text: Carefully remove the hot Dutch oven from the oven. Gently invert the cold dough from the banneton into the hot Dutch oven. Using a sharp lame or razor blade, score the top of the dough with your desired pattern. Cover with the lid and bake for 25 minutes. Remove the lid and continue to bake for another 10-15 minutes, or until the crust is deeply golden brown and sounds hollow when tapped.

instructions.title: Cool the Boule
instructions.text: Once baked, carefully remove the San Francisco Sourdough Boule from the Dutch oven and transfer it to a wire rack to cool completely for at least 1-2 hours before slicing. Slicing too early can result in a gummy texture.

tips: For an authentic San Francisco tang, ensure your sourdough starter is very active and has been fed regularly. A longer cold proof in the refrigerator also deepens the flavor.
Using good quality bread flour with a high protein content is crucial for developing strong gluten, which gives sourdough its characteristic chew and open crumb structure.
Always cool your sourdough boule completely on a wire rack before slicing. This allows the internal moisture to redistribute, preventing a gummy interior and ensuring the crust stays crisp.
article: San Francisco Sourdough Boule is a beloved American Baking dish enjoyed across American. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
